You are the execution engine for Brain OS.

Your role is to help plan, prioritize, execute, and review work across all projects using the application's structured data.

Always treat MongoDB as the source of truth. Before making recommendations, review the project's PRD, current state, blockers, tasks, deliverables, goals, notes, reviews, and execution history.

projects.prd:
Objective

Build a full-stack MERN Personal Operating System that centralizes knowledge management, planning, project execution, task tracking, reviews, goals, deliverables, and context into a single application.

Core Principles

• MongoDB is the source of truth.
• React is a CRUD-only interface.
• Express provides REST APIs.
• External AI (Codex CLI) performs reasoning and planning.
• No AI pipelines run inside the web application.

Core Modules

• Dashboard
• Notes
• Tasks
• Deliverables
• Projects
• Goals
• Ideas
• Context
• Reviews
• Day Plans

Project Execution Loop

1. Capture project definition.
2. Store PRD and execution context.
3. Maintain actionable next steps.
4. Codex reads project context.
5. Generate daily work.
6. Review progress.
7. Repeat until production ready.

Success Metrics

• Fast CRUD experience.
• Structured personal knowledge.
• Reliable project tracking.
• AI-ready structured data.
• Maintainable and extensible architecture.
projects.problemStatement:
Current personal productivity tools scatter knowledge, tasks, projects, reviews, and planning across multiple applications, making it difficult to maintain context, execute consistently, and leverage AI effectively.

Brain OS solves this by acting as a centralized Personal Operating System where MongoDB becomes long-term memory, React provides a fast CRUD interface, and external AI agents (Codex CLI) reason over structured data without being tightly coupled to the web application.

The goal is to create a single source of truth that supports planning, execution, reflection, and continuous improvement.

# Product Requirements Document

## Objective

Build a premium restaurant website and online ordering platform for Ama's Kitchen that showcases authentic Ghanaian cuisine while providing a fast, intuitive, and mobile-friendly ordering experience.

## Target Users

* New customers discovering the restaurant
* Returning customers placing repeat orders
* Customers browsing menus before visiting
* Customers ordering food for pickup or delivery

## Core Features

* Beautiful landing page
* Responsive navigation
* Food categories
* Interactive menu
* Shopping cart
* Quantity management
* Online ordering
* Services section
* Restaurant information
* Contact section
* Mobile-first responsive design

## Success Metrics

* Fast page load
* Smooth ordering experience
* High mobile usability
* Increased online orders
* Premium brand presentation
* Easy menu management
projects.problemStatement:
Many Ghanaian restaurants struggle to provide a modern online ordering experience that reflects the quality of their food. Customers often rely on phone calls, WhatsApp messages, or third-party delivery platforms that reduce profit margins and provide little control over branding or customer experience.

Ama's Kitchen addresses this by providing a modern web platform where customers can browse authentic Ghanaian dishes, manage their cart, place orders seamlessly, and experience the restaurant's brand through a premium digital experience.

# Brain Update — AI Development Roadmap

## Projects / Active Work

* Improve the development workflow with AI-assisted tooling.
* Build an AI Codebase Analyzer for architectural analysis and optimization.
* Create a project-scoped Code Reviewer subagent.

## Tasks

* Connect the platform to Telegram.
* Route platform messages to Telegram.
* Support mentorship subscription through Telegram.
* Explore Model Context Protocol (MCP).
* Experiment with Context7 MCP.
* Experiment with Neon MCP.
* Experiment with Playwright MCP.
* Add Playwright to the frontend development workflow.
* Install Playwright MCP.
* Integrate browser automation into the development workflow.
* Add vision capabilities to the development workflow.
* Learn how to create reusable Claude/Codex skills.
* Learn how to trigger custom commands.
* Build the AI Codebase Analyzer.
* Create the Code Reviewer subagent.
* Create the Explore subagent.

## Ideas

* Create reusable custom commands such as:

  * `/review`
  * `/commit`
  * `/explore`
* Build an AI Codebase Analyzer that can:

  * Map project data flow.
  * Detect architectural issues.
  * Identify conflicting execution paths.
  * Detect duplicated logic.
  * Suggest performance improvements.
  * Highlight optimization opportunities.
* Use Playwright MCP to:

  * Open projects automatically.
  * Navigate application UIs.
  * Interact with components.
  * Execute frontend tests.
* Build an Explore subagent capable of answering questions such as:

  * "Map the data flow in this project."
  * "Where does the data come from?"
  * "Which components consume it?"
* Design the Code Reviewer subagent to:

  * Detect dead code.
  * Detect unused imports.
  * Detect `console.log` statements.
  * Detect missing React `key` props.
  * Detect accessibility issues (missing `alt` and `aria-label`).
  * Detect hardcoded values that should be constants or environment variables.
  * Detect violations of project conventions defined in `CLAUDE.md`.
  * Produce a read-only Markdown report grouped by severity.
  * Support trigger phrases such as "Review my code", "Run the reviewer", and `/code-reviewer`.

## Research

* Claude Skills.
* Codex custom commands.
* Model Context Protocol (MCP).
* Context7 MCP.
* Neon MCP.
* Playwright MCP.

## Follow-ups

* Decide how Telegram integration should authenticate users.
* Define the architecture for reusable AI subagents.
* Determine how project-specific rules (such as `CLAUDE.md`) should be loaded into the Code Reviewer.

## Ideas

- Explore MCP tools:
  - Context7 MCP
  - Neon MCP
  - Playwright MCP
- Install and test Playwright MCP.
- Add Playwright into frontend workflow.
- Learn how Claude skills/custom commands work.
- Define custom commands such as:
  - /review
  - /commit
  - /explore
  - /code-reviewer

## Code Reviewer Subagent Spec
Create a project-scoped subagent called `code-reviewer`.

It should review current uncommitted changes and check for:
- Dead code
- Unused imports
- console.log statements
- Missing React key props
- Accessibility issues:
  - Missing alt text
  - Missing aria-labels on icon buttons
- Hardcoded values that should be env vars or constants
- Anything that breaks patterns in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md

Output:
- Markdown report
- Findings grouped by severity
- Read-only: no edits

Triggers:
- "review my code"
- "run the reviewer"
- /code-reviewer

## Context
I want Brain OS notes to act as raw memory. When I run `update brain`, these notes should be classified into projects, tasks, ideas, and context without generating a day plan.

## Code Reviewer Subagent Spec
Create a project-scoped subagent called `code-reviewer`.

It should review current uncommitted changes and check for:
- Dead code
- Unused imports
- console.log statements
- Missing React key props
- Accessibility issues:
  - Missing alt text
  - Missing aria-labels on icon buttons
- Hardcoded values that should be env vars or constants
- Anything that breaks patterns in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md

Output:
- Markdown report
- Findings grouped by severity
- Read-only: no edits

Triggers:
- "review my code"
- "run the reviewer"
- /code-reviewer

## Context
I want Brain OS notes to act as raw memory. When I run `update brain`, these notes should be classified into projects, tasks, ideas, and context without generating a day plan.

# Brain Update — AI Development Roadmap

## Projects / Active Work

* Integrate Telegram into the platform to centralize communication and mentorship subscriptions.
* Improve the development workflow with AI-assisted tooling.
* Build an AI Codebase Analyzer for architectural analysis and optimization.
* Create a project-scoped Code Reviewer subagent.
* Build an Explore subagent for understanding unfamiliar codebases.

## Tasks

* Connect the platform to Telegram.
* Route platform messages to Telegram.
* Support mentorship subscription through Telegram.
* Explore Model Context Protocol (MCP).
* Experiment with Context7 MCP.
* Experiment with Neon MCP.
* Experiment with Playwright MCP.
* Add Playwright to the frontend development workflow.
* Install Playwright MCP.
* Integrate browser automation into the development workflow.
* Add vision capabilities to the development workflow.
* Learn how to create reusable Claude/Codex skills.
* Learn how to trigger custom commands.
* Build the AI Codebase Analyzer.
* Create the Code Reviewer subagent.
* Create the Explore subagent.

## Ideas

* Create reusable custom commands such as:

  * `/review`
  * `/commit`
  * `/explore`
* Build an AI Codebase Analyzer that can:

  * Map project data flow.
  * Detect architectural issues.
  * Identify conflicting execution paths.
  * Detect duplicated logic.
  * Suggest performance improvements.
  * Highlight optimization opportunities.
* Use Playwright MCP to:

  * Open projects automatically.
  * Navigate application UIs.
  * Interact with components.
  * Execute frontend tests.
* Build an Explore subagent capable of answering questions such as:

  * "Map the data flow in this project."
  * "Where does the data come from?"
  * "Which components consume it?"
* Design the Code Reviewer subagent to:

  * Detect dead code.
  * Detect unused imports.
  * Detect `console.log` statements.
  * Detect missing React `key` props.
  * Detect accessibility issues (missing `alt` and `aria-label`).
  * Detect hardcoded values that should be constants or environment variables.
  * Detect violations of project conventions defined in `CLAUDE.md`.
  * Produce a read-only Markdown report grouped by severity.
  * Support trigger phrases such as "Review my code", "Run the reviewer", and `/code-reviewer`.

## Research

* Claude Skills.
* Codex custom commands.
* Model Context Protocol (MCP).
* Context7 MCP.
* Neon MCP.
* Playwright MCP.

## Follow-ups

* Decide how Telegram integration should authenticate users.
* Define the architecture for reusable AI subagents.
* Determine how project-specific rules (such as `CLAUDE.md`) should be loaded into the Code Reviewer.

Brain Update — Brain App SaaS Vision

Context

* The long-term goal is to make the Brain app reusable by anyone.
* The ultimate vision is to turn the Brain app into a SaaS product.
* The MongoDB database should eventually become the application’s long-term memory.
* The application should function as a structured productivity system, similar in concept to an AI coding assistant, but focused on productivity and knowledge management.

Projects / Active Work

* Brain App

Tasks

* Audit the codebase.
* Extract system prompts from the application.
* Separate personal information from reusable application logic.
* Make the application reusable by other users.
* Design a JSON format for loading a knowledge base (KB).

Ideas

* Users should primarily load their own context into MongoDB.
* Use a JSON import mechanism to populate the knowledge base.

Decisions

* The goal is to evolve the Brain app into a SaaS application.
* MongoDB will become the application’s memory layer.

- List concrete implementation tasks without changing code unless explicitly asked.
